items.title: Victor Wembanyama played 42-minutes in game seven.
items.author: Hoop Informatics
items.body_excerpt:
Victor Wembanyama played 42-minutes in game seven. 

In half court situations with Wemby on the court, the Thunder made only one basket at the rim.

PivotFade.com

images.alt:
Thunder shot chart in half court situations with Wemby on the court showing only rim attempts with red dots being misses and green dot showing the one make. 

Thunder 1/5 20% at the rim in this scenario

items.title: Big dichotomy in team building in this Spurs-Knicks finals. The Spurs are here because of a No. 1 pick, a No. 2, a No. 4, a No. 9, and a trade for De'Aaron Fox. The Knicks are only the 2nd team since 2000 to make the finals without one of its 1st round picks in their starting lin
items.author: Mike Vorkunov
items.body_excerpt: Big dichotomy in team building in this Spurs-Knicks finals. The Spurs are here because of a No. 1 pick, a No. 2, a No. 4, a No. 9, and a trade for De'Aaron Fox. The Knicks are only the 2nd team since 2000 to make the finals without one of its 1st round picks in their starting lineup.

Luigi Suigo is likely Villanova-bound, but I still wanted to evaluate the "Italian Wemby."

items.title: The team-building differences between the Knicks and Spurs are so drastic.
items.author: Jared Dubin
items.body_excerpt:
The team-building differences between the Knicks and Spurs are so drastic.

Drafted players have gotten over 60% of the playoff minutes for the Spurs, but it's only 19% for the Knicks. If you cut out the garbage time guys, it's 65% for SAS and 14% for NYK. Pretty wild.

items.title: During NY’s current 11-game winning streak, Mikal Bridges is averaging 16.6 points while slashing 63/38/100%
items.author: Tommy Beer
items.body_excerpt:
During NY’s current 11-game winning streak, Mikal Bridges is averaging 16.6 points while slashing 63/38/100%

Per @basketball-reference.com, he is the only player in NBA history to average more than 15 PPG while shooting > 60% from the floor and > 90% on FT's over an 11-game postseason stretch

items.title: May 31, 1999: Sean Elliott's sixth 3-pointer is the game-winner for San Antonio over Portland in Game 2 of the Western Conference Finals.
items.author: NBA Cobwebs
items.body_excerpt:
May 31, 1999: Sean Elliott's sixth 3-pointer is the game-winner for San Antonio over Portland in Game 2 of the Western Conference Finals.

Elliott scored 22 PTS and Tim Duncan had 23 PTS/10 REB/5 BLK in the Spurs' 86-85 win. The Blazers' Damon Stoudamire had 15 PTS/7 AST/6 REB.
